findings.title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms.
findings.claim: VIP disinhibitory interneurons in mouse cortex linearly scale local gamma power without altering its stimulus tuning, while suppressing long-range gamma coherence between regions processing non-matched stimuli.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Combining electrophysiology, optogenetics, and computational modeling, we found that VIP disinhibitory interneurons in mouse cortex linearly scale gamma power locally without changing its stimulus tuning. Conversely, they suppress long-range synchronization when two regions process non-matched stimuli, tuning gamma coherence globally.
findings.effect_size: VIP cells linearly scale gamma power locally and globally tune gamma coherence between regions
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of VIP cells in mouse V1 modulates gamma power locally and inter-areal gamma coherence; computational model with VIP→SST inhibition reproduces this dual role.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP disinhibitory interneurons in mouse cortex linearly scale gamma power locally without changing its stimulus tuning.
findings.study_system: mouse primary visual cortex (V1) — in vivo optogenetics + LFP, plus computational model

findings.title: VIP interneuron activity during sleep conveys the cortical infraslow oscillation.
findings.claim: Cortical layer 2/3 VIP interneurons display a ~0.02-Hz infraslow oscillation that is inversely phase-coupled to infraslow spindle activity during slow-wave sleep, a coupling absent in PV and SST interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Notably, across all states, VIP activity showed a profound ∼0.02-Hz infraslow rhythm, which, during SWS epochs, was inversely phase-coupled to the infraslow rhythm of spindle activity. In contrast, such coupling was absent in SST and PV interneurons, suggesting that VIP cells uniquely regulate infraslow spindle-related dynamics within cortical circuits.
findings.effect_size: ~0.02-Hz infraslow VIP rhythm; VIP activity acutely upregulated during spindles/SOs, lowest during SWS overall
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging of layer 2/3 VIP, PV, SST interneurons in naturally sleeping mice across wake/SWS/REM and during spindles/slow oscillations.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP activity was lowest during SWS and acutely upregulated during spindles and SOs, presumably facilitating memory processing in cortical circuits.
findings.study_system: naturally sleeping male mice, layer 2/3 cortex, two-photon Ca2+ imaging

findings.title: Multisensory gamma stimulation promotes glymphatic clearance of amyloid.
findings.claim: Chemogenetic inhibition of cortical VIP interneurons attenuates 40-Hz multisensory gamma stimulation–induced amyloid clearance in 5XFAD mice without affecting baseline 40-Hz LFP power, indicating VIP cells couple gamma activity to glymphatic peptide release.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To causally determine whether VIP interneurons regulate amyloid clearance during gamma stimulation, we chemogenetically inhibited VIP interneurons using PHP.eb.AAV::Syn:DIO-hM4Di-mCherry in VIP-Cre 5XFAD transgenic mice. Chemogenetically inhibiting VIP neurons before gamma stimulation attenuated amyloid clearance (Fig.
findings.effect_size: n=7-8 mice/group; two-way ANOVA, Fisher's LSD
findings.evidence: VIP-Cre × 5XFAD mice with hM4Di-DREADD silencing of VIP cells abolished amyloid clearance benefit of 40-Hz stimulation; no change in baseline 40 Hz power.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Quantification of amyloid in experiment represented in b (n = 7 (no stimulation + tdTomato), 8 (40 Hz + tdTomato), 7 (no stimulation + hM4Di) and 7 (40 Hz + hM4Di) VIP-Cre 5XFAD mice; data are mean ± s.e.m.; P values by two-way ANOVA followed by Fisher's LSD test).
findings.study_system: VIP-Cre 5XFAD mouse cortex; chemogenetics + 40-Hz multisensory stimulation; in vivo electrophysiology and amyloid IHC

findings.title: A microcircuit model involving parvalbumin, somatostatin, and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide inhibitory interneurons for the modulation of neuronal oscillation during visual processing.
findings.claim: In a biophysical V1 layer 2/3 microcircuit model, PV and SST interneurons preferentially induce gamma (30–80 Hz) and beta (20–30 Hz) firing of pyramidal neurons respectively, while rapid VIP→SST inhibition is required for attentional modulation of low-gamma (30–50 Hz) power.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In simulations with our model, inhibitory signals from PV and SOM neurons preferentially induced neuronal firing at gamma (30-80 Hz) and beta (20-30 Hz) frequencies, respectively, in agreement with observed physiological results. Furthermore, our model indicated that rapid inhibition from VIP to SOM subtypes underlies marked attentional modulation for low-gamma frequency (30-50 Hz) in Pyr neuron responses.
findings.effect_size: VIP→SST synaptic weight WVIP-SOM=0.5 abolishes attentional gamma modulation; delay dVIP-SOM modulates low-gamma magnitude
findings.evidence: Computational simulations of a Pyr-PV-SST-VIP V1 microcircuit; sweep over VIP→SST synaptic weight and delay reproduces frequency-band-specific roles.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: the attentional effects of STH power for the gamma-band activity of Pyr neurons was not observed in the model simulation with WVIP-SOM of 0.5
findings.study_system: biophysical computational model of mouse V1 layers 2/3

findings.title: Rate and oscillatory switching dynamics of a multilayer visual microcircuit model.
findings.claim: A two-layer biophysical model of mouse V1 shows that VIP-, PV-, and SST-cell mutual inhibition implements bistable switches that toggle pyramidal cells between low-firing/low-frequency (SST-dominated) and high-firing/high-frequency (disinhibited) oscillatory states, with layer-specific differences in frequency and power.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Two mutual inhibitory motifs that include SST, PV, and VIP cells serve as ultrasensitive or bistable switches with different sensitivity, which can toggle the microcircuit between the two states.
findings.effect_size: External drive to VIP cells increases oscillation frequency; VIP silencing only slightly raises frequency
findings.evidence: Rate-based and spiking computational model parameterized for L2/3 and L5 mouse V1 reproducing oscillatory state transitions.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Both input to VIP and PV cells strongly increased the frequency of the dominant oscillation in superficial and deep layers, whereas the power of the oscillation peak generally decreased (Figure 5A–D).
findings.study_system: computational two-layer V1 microcircuit model (mouse)

findings.title: The gamma rhythm as a guardian of brain health.
findings.claim: A theoretical framework (GAMER) proposes that four classes of interneurons—PV, VIP, SST, and NOS—exploit endogenous cortical gamma oscillations to perform active vasomotor and homeostatic control, linking gamma rhythms to circuit maintenance and brain health.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We propose that four classes of interneurons, namely those expressing parvalbumin (PV), vasointestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST), and nitric oxide synthase (NOS) take advantage of endogenous gamma to perform active vasomotor control that maintains homeostasis in the neuronal tissue.
findings.evidence: Review and synthesis of gamma rhythm mechanisms, gamma entrainment using sensory stimulation (GENUS) literature, and proposal of vasomotor coupling.
findings.study_system: theoretical/review across rodent and human cortex

findings.title: Synaptic Mechanisms Underlying the Network State-Dependent Recruitment of VIP-Expressing Interneurons in the CA1 Hippocampus.
findings.claim: In awake mouse hippocampus, VIP interneuron-selective (VIP-IS) cells fire preferentially during theta-run epochs at the rising phase and peak of the theta cycle but are NOT recruited during sharp-wave ripples, contradicting model predictions for ripple recruitment.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Two-photon Ca2+-imaging in awake mice supported in part the theoretical predictions, revealing a significant speed modulation of VIP-IS cells and their preferential albeit delayed recruitment during theta-run epochs, with estimated firing at the rising phase and peak of the theta cycle. However, it also uncovered that VIP-IS cells are not activated during ripples.
findings.effect_size: VIP-IS cells fire at rising phase/peak of theta, absent during ripples
findings.evidence: Combined optophysiological recordings, two-photon Ca2+ imaging in awake mice, and biophysical computational modeling.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: estimated firing at the rising phase and peak of the theta cycle. However, it also uncovered that VIP-IS cells are not activated during ripples.
findings.study_system: awake mouse CA1 hippocampus, two-photon imaging + slice + model

findings.title: VIP Interneurons Contribute to Avoidance Behavior by Regulating Information Flow across Hippocampal-Prefrontal Networks.
findings.claim: Prefrontal VIP interneuron activity gates hippocampal-prefrontal theta synchrony: optogenetic inhibition of VIP cells decreases open-arm avoidance specifically when hippocampal-prefrontal theta coherence is strong, linking VIP disinhibition to inter-areal theta coordination.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Indeed, inhibiting VIP interneurons disrupts network-level representations of the open arms and decreases open arm avoidance specifically when hippocampal-prefrontal theta synchrony is strong.
findings.effect_size: VIP inhibition decreases open-arm avoidance only at high theta synchrony states
findings.evidence: Optogenetic inhibition of VIP cells in mPFC during elevated plus-maze with simultaneous mPFC-vHPC LFP recordings.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: inhibiting VIP interneurons disrupts network-level representations of the open arms and decreases open arm avoidance specifically when hippocampal-prefrontal theta synchrony is strong.
findings.study_system: mouse medial prefrontal cortex; in vivo electrophysiology + optogenetics; elevated plus maze

findings.title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
findings.claim: VIP+/calretinin+ type-3 interneuron-specific (IS3) cells in CA1 oriens/alveus selectively innervate oriens-lacunosum moleculare (OLM) cells via dendritic synapses; synchronous IS3 spikes control OLM firing rate and spike timing, implicating IS3 cells in temporal patterning of theta-rhythmic OLM output.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Moreover, the synchronous generation of a single spike in several IS cells that converged onto a single OLM controlled the firing rate and timing of OLM interneurons.
findings.effect_size: single-spike convergent IS input controls OLM firing rate/timing
findings.evidence: Dual patch-clamp + targeted optogenetics in acute mouse hippocampal slices; unitary IPSC analysis at IS3-OLM synapses.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: the synchronous generation of a single spike in several IS cells that converged onto a single OLM controlled the firing rate and timing of OLM interneurons.
findings.study_system: acute mouse hippocampal CA1 slices; paired patch-clamp + ChR2 optogenetics

findings.title: Using computational models to predict in vivo synaptic inputs to interneuron specific 3 (IS3) cells of CA1 hippocampus that also allow their recruitment during rhythmic states.
findings.claim: Multi-compartment computational models of hippocampal VIP+ IS3 cells predict that balanced excitation and inhibition (with low numbers of correlated synapses) produce asynchronous in-vivo-like states sensitive to 8-Hz theta-timed inputs, generating strongly increased theta-band spectral power.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that asynchronous, in vivo-like states that are sensitive to additional theta-timed inputs (8 Hz) exist when excitatory and inhibitory synaptic conductances are approximately equally balanced and with low numbers of activated synapses receiving correlated inputs. Incoming theta-timed inputs result in strongly increased spectral power relative to baseline.
findings.effect_size: theta-timed inputs increase spectral power; balanced conductances yield asynchronous baseline state
findings.evidence: Large-scale parameter exploration of multi-compartment IS3 models with optimized synaptic conductances and 8-Hz layer-specific inputs.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Incoming theta-timed inputs result in strongly increased spectral power relative to baseline.
findings.study_system: multi-compartment computational models of hippocampal VIP+ IS3 cells

findings.title: Basolateral amygdala oscillations enable fear learning in a biophysical model.
findings.claim: A biophysical model of basolateral amygdala demonstrates that PV, SST, and VIP interneurons each contribute essential rhythm-generating roles (low/high theta and gamma); low theta (~3–6 Hz) emerges as a biomarker of successful fear conditioning via STDP-shaped fear circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we create a biophysically detailed model of the BLA circuit to show that several classes of interneurons (PV, SOM, and VIP) in the BLA can be critically involved in producing the rhythms; these rhythms promote the formation of a dedicated fear circuit shaped through spike-timing-dependent plasticity. We find that the low theta rhythm is a biomarker of successful fear conditioning.
findings.effect_size: low theta (~3-6 Hz) is biomarker of fear conditioning
findings.evidence: Hodgkin-Huxley biophysical BLA model with PV/SST/VIP interneurons; STDP applied to inputs; comparison to LFP rhythm bands.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We find that the low theta rhythm is a biomarker of successful fear conditioning.
findings.study_system: biophysical computational model of basolateral amygdala

findings.title: Endogenous VIP VPAC<sub>1</sub> Receptor Activation Modulates Hippocampal Theta Burst Induced LTP: Transduction Pathways and GABAergic Mechanisms.
findings.claim: In CA1 of young adult Wistar rats, endogenous VIP acting through VPAC1 receptors (but not VPAC2) inhibits theta-burst-stimulation–induced LTP via GABAergic disinhibition, regulating CaMKII autophosphorylation and Kv4.2 channel phosphorylation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Endogenous VIP, acting on VPAC1 (but not VPAC2) receptors, inhibited CA1 hippocampal LTP induced by TBS in young adult Wistar rats and this effect was dependent on GABAergic transmission and relied on the integrity of NMDA and CaMKII-dependent LTP expression mechanisms but not on PKA and PKC activity.
findings.effect_size: VPAC1 antagonism prevents VIP inhibition of TBS-LTP
findings.evidence: Hippocampal slice electrophysiology with VPAC1/VPAC2 selective antagonists; biochemical Western blot for CaMKII/GluA1/Kv4.2 phosphorylation.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Endogenous VIP, acting on VPAC1 (but not VPAC2) receptors, inhibited CA1 hippocampal LTP induced by TBS in young adult Wistar rats
findings.study_system: young-adult Wistar rat CA1 hippocampal slices, theta-burst stimulation

findings.title: Cortical networks with multiple interneuron types generate oscillatory patterns during predictive coding.
findings.claim: In a hierarchical cortical predictive-coding model with PV/SST/VIP cell-type-specific connectivity, SST and VIP interneurons control oscillation amplitude and number of cycles between representation and error neurons in opposing directions; in-silico VIP silencing diminishes oscillatory patterns by removing disinhibition of SST.
findings.claim_source_sentence: while PV provides a blanket of inhibition dampening network activity, SST and VIP serve to control the amplitude of neural oscillations and the number of cycles between representation and error neurons in opposing directions.
findings.effect_size: VIP silencing diminishes oscillatory patterns; SST and VIP push oscillation amplitude in opposite directions
findings.evidence: Two-area predictive-coding network model with naturalistic image inputs; optogenetics-inspired silencing simulations.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: optogenetic silencing of VIP neurons diminished oscillatory patterns, reflecting disruption of their disinhibitory effect on SST cells and supressing local oscillations.
findings.study_system: hierarchical computational predictive-coding model with E-PV-SST-VIP microcircuits

findings.title: Computational modeling of ketamine-induced changes in gamma-band oscillations: The contribution of parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons.
findings.claim: In a layer-2/3 cortical model, reducing NMDA-R activity selectively on PV or SST (but not VIP) interneurons reproduces ketamine-like increases in gamma-band power; complementary MEG analysis (n=12) showed flatter aperiodic slope and increased gamma correlated with regional PV and GRIN2D expression.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Computational modeling revealed that reduced NMDA-R activity in parvalbumin or somatostatin interneurons could reproduce increased gamma-band power by increasing pyramidal neuron firing rate, but did not account for changes in the aperiodic slope.
findings.effect_size: MEG n=12; gamma-band 30-90 Hz; flatter aperiodic slope after ketamine
findings.evidence: MEG resting-state n=12 + neural-mass cortical model with PV/SST/VIP cell-type NMDA-R manipulations.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We analyzed resting-state MEG data from n = 12 participants who were administered Ketamine to assess changes in gamma-band (30-90 Hz) power and the slope of the aperiodic power spectrum compared to placebo.
findings.study_system: human MEG (n=12) + cortical layer-2/3 computational model

findings.title: Dysfunctional neural dynamics associated with sensory phenotypes in Fragile X syndrome: insights from mouse models.
findings.claim: In Fragile X syndrome models, dysfunctional VIP+ interneurons correlate with elevated susceptibility to irrelevant stimuli, and Fmr1-KO mice exhibit elevated broadband gamma EEG power and abnormal phase-locking to gamma-modulated acoustic stimuli, paralleling human FXS phenotypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: They found that awake and freely moving adult Fmr1 KO mice exhibited elevated broadband gamma power in resting EEGs under both still (not moving) and movement conditions compared to wild-type (WT) mice. Given the influence of VIP+ cells on circuit function and behavior, one study found a correlation between dysfunction in VIP+ cells and susceptibility to irrelevant stimuli in Fmr1 KO mice [117].
findings.effect_size: elevated broadband gamma power in Fmr1 KO awake EEG (still and movement)
findings.evidence: Review of Fmr1-KO mouse and human FXS EEG/MEG and circuit studies linking VIP-cell dysfunction to gamma-band abnormalities.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: awake and freely moving adult Fmr1 KO mice exhibited elevated broadband gamma power in resting EEGs under both still (not moving) and movement conditions compared to wild-type (WT) mice.
findings.study_system: review/synthesis: Fmr1-KO mice and human FXS EEG

findings.title: Synaptic plasticity facilitates oscillations in a V1 cortical column model with multiple interneuron types.
findings.claim: A data-constrained mouse V1 spiking network with PV/SST/VIP cells lacks intrinsic rhythms but generates broad-band 15–60 Hz oscillations only after STDP-driven structural plasticity; feedforward inputs enhance and feedback inputs suppress these rhythms, and oscillation generation requires all four cell types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The resulting model matched in vivo cell-type-specific firing rates for spontaneous and stimulus-evoked conditions in mice, although rhythmic activity was absent. Upon introduction of long-term synaptic plasticity in the form of an STDP rule, broad-band (15-60 Hz) oscillations emerged, with feedforward/feedback input streams enhancing/suppressing the oscillatory drive, respectively. These plasticity-triggered rhythms relied on all cell types, and specific experience-dependent connectivity patterns were required to generate oscillations.
findings.effect_size: oscillations emerge in 15–60 Hz band only after plasticity
findings.evidence: Spiking network simulation calibrated to mouse V1 firing rates; STDP added; oscillation power evaluated.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: broad-band (15-60 Hz) oscillations emerged, with feedforward/feedback input streams enhancing/suppressing the oscillatory drive, respectively.
findings.study_system: biophysically constrained mouse V1 spiking column model

findings.title: Connectivity and network state-dependent recruitment of long-range VIP-GABAergic neurons in the mouse hippocampus.
findings.claim: A class of long-range VIP+ projection neurons (VIP-LRPs) connecting CA1 to subiculum decreases activity during theta-run epochs, is more active during quiet wakefulness, and is not coupled to sharp-wave ripples in awake mice—contrasting with local VIP-IS cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In awake mice, VIP-LRPs decrease their activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness but not coupled to sharp-wave ripples.
findings.effect_size: VIP-LRPs: decreased activity during theta-run, increased during quiet wake, no SWR coupling
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging, anatomical tracing, and slice patch-clamp in awake mice; comparison across brain states.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP-LRPs decrease their activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness but not coupled to sharp-wave ripples.
findings.study_system: awake mouse CA1-subiculum, VIP-Cre two-photon imaging + slice

findings.title: Neuronal cell-subtype specificity of neural synchronization in mouse primary visual cortex.
findings.claim: In mouse V1 superficial layers, neuron-network coupling distributions are cell-subtype specific: PV+ and VIP+ interneurons show skewed distributions toward strong coupling with the local population, while SST+ cells split into two subpopulations with distinct coupling profiles.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Parvalbumin (PV)- and Vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing INs exhibit skewed distributions towards strong network-coupling; in Somatostatin (SST)-expressing INs, however, two physiological subpopulations are identified with distinct neuron-network coupling profiles.
findings.effect_size: PV and VIP show skewed distributions toward strong coupling; SST splits into two subpopulations
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging combined with 2P-targeted whole-cell patch-clamp in mouse V1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Parvalbumin (PV)- and Vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing INs exhibit skewed distributions towards strong network-coupling
findings.study_system: mouse V1 superficial layers, in vivo two-photon imaging + targeted patch

findings.title: Mechanisms of Dominant Electrophysiological Features of Four Subtypes of Layer 1 Interneurons.
findings.claim: A biophysical model of neocortical layer-1 VIP+ interneurons (and α7 cells) shows intrinsic resonance with theta/alpha-band input near resting potential, enabled by T-type Ca2+ channel dynamics—providing an intrinsic mechanism for cortical VIP-cell engagement at theta/alpha frequencies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We further test the frequency-dependent response of the VIP cell model through a dynamic gain (DG) analysis, and find that the VIP cell model can resonate with alpha/theta band input, enabled by the dynamics of the T-type Ca2+ channel.
findings.effect_size: VIP and α7 cell models resonate with theta/alpha-band input only near resting potential
findings.evidence: Slice patch-clamp characterization of L1 IN subtypes + extended exponential integrate-and-fire computational modeling.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The VIP model resonates with theta/alpha band input only when the average input is around the resting.
findings.study_system: mouse neocortex layer 1 IN slice + computational model

findings.title: Excitatory/inhibitory ratio disruption modulates neural synchrony and flow directions in a cortical microcircuit.
findings.claim: In a layer-2/3 V1 microcircuit model, decreasing PV population size shifts PV activity to precede pyramidal activity and enhances beta and gamma oscillations, while decreasing SST shifts pyramidal-to-PV ordering and selectively impairs gamma; VIP changes alter information flow between Pyr and PV populations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that when the E/I balance is disrupted by decreasing the PV population size, activity of the PV population precedes that of the pyramidal population, which enhances beta and gamma oscillations. Conversely, pyramidal neuronal population activity was the precursor of PV interneuron activity when the E/I imbalance was induced by decreasing the SOM population size; this preferentially impaired gamma-frequency activity.
findings.effect_size: PV reduction enhances beta+gamma; SST reduction impairs gamma
findings.evidence: V1 layer 2/3 spiking microcircuit model; population sizes systematically varied; spectral and Granger-causality–style flow analyses.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: this preferentially impaired gamma-frequency activity
findings.study_system: V1 layer 2/3 spiking microcircuit computational model

findings.title: Novel long-range inhibitory nNOS-expressing hippocampal cells.
findings.claim: Hippocampal long-range nNOS+ inhibitory cells (LINCs), distinct from VIP and SST cells, project to extrahippocampal regions and modulate CA1 oscillations and interregional coherence, illustrating that VIP-negative inhibitory long-range projection cells also shape hippocampal rhythms.
findings.claim_source_sentence: LINCs are thus both interneurons and projection neurons. LINCs display regular spiking non-pyramidal firing patterns, are primarily located in the stratum oriens or pyramidale, have sparsely spiny dendrites, and do not typically express somatostatin, VIP, or the muscarinic acetylcholine receptor M2. We further demonstrate that LINCs can strongly influence hippocampal function and oscillations, including interregional coherence.
findings.effect_size: LINC manipulation alters hippocampal oscillations and interregional coherence
findings.evidence: Intersectional viral genetics, anatomical tracing, slice electrophysiology, and chemogenetic manipulation in mice; in vivo LFP coherence.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: LINCs can strongly influence hippocampal function and oscillations, including interregional coherence.
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampal CA1 + extrahippocampal targets; in vivo LFP

findings.title: Multiple distinct subtypes of GABAergic neurons in mouse visual cortex identified by triple immunostaining
findings.claim: Mouse visual cortex contains at least 13 molecularly distinct GABAergic interneuron groups, with VIP intersecting with CR (CR+VIP), SOM (SOM+VIP), and ChAT (VIP+ChAT)—providing the molecular substrate for cell-type-specific roles in cortical oscillations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We have found at least 13 distinct groups of GABAergic neurons which include PV, CR, SOM, CCK (cholecystokinin), CR + SOM, CR + NPY (neuropeptide Y), CR + VIP (vasointestinal polypeptide), SOM + NPY, SOM + VIP, VIP + ChAT (choline acetyltransferase), CCK + NPY, CR + SOM + NPY and CR + SOM + VIP expressing cells.
findings.effect_size: 13 distinct GABAergic groups identified
findings.evidence: Triple immunohistochemistry across mouse V1 with antibodies for PV, CR, SOM, CCK, NPY, VIP, ChAT.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We have found at least 13 distinct groups of GABAergic neurons
findings.study_system: mouse visual cortex (V1), triple IHC

findings.title: Vigilance and Behavioral State-Dependent Modulation of Cortical Neuronal Activity throughout the Sleep/Wake Cycle.
findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex layer 2/3, VIP interneurons are most active during REM sleep, PV cells fire highest during both REM and NREM (with rapid decrease at wake onset), and SST activity is stable across the sleep-wake cycle; PV and most VIP cells are modulated by delta and theta LFP oscillations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: we found that PV action potential firing activity was largest during both rapid eye movement (REM) and nonrapid eye movement (NREM) sleep stages, that VIP neurons were most active during REM sleep, and that the overall activity of SST neurons remained stable throughout the sleep/wake cycle. Simultaneous local field potential (LFP) recordings further revealed that except for SST neurons, a large proportion of neurons were modulated by ongoing delta and theta oscillations.
findings.effect_size: VIP highest during REM; PV highest during REM and NREM; SST stable; PV and SST modulated by delta/theta
findings.evidence: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging + targeted loose-patch/whole-cell recordings + LFP in naturally sleeping head-restrained mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP neurons were most active during REM sleep, and that the overall activity of SST neurons remained stable throughout the sleep/wake cycle.
findings.study_system: naturally sleeping head-restrained mice; layer 2/3 primary somatosensory (barrel) cortex

findings.title: Top-down input modulates visual context processing through an interneuron-specific circuit.
findings.claim: In mouse V1 during an oddball paradigm, ACa–V1 synchrony peaks in the theta/alpha band (~10 Hz); 10-Hz optogenetic stimulation of ACa→V1 inputs activates V1 VIP and inhibits SST cells, and chemogenetic VIP silencing abolishes both ACa–V1 theta/alpha synchrony and V1 deviance detection.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Local field potential recordings in mice during an oddball paradigm showed that ACa-V1 synchrony peaks in the theta/alpha band (≈10 Hz). Chemogenetic inhibition of V1-VIPs disrupted Aca-V1 synchrony and deviance detection in the V1.
findings.effect_size: ACa-V1 synchrony ~10 Hz; chemogenetic VIP silencing abolishes synchrony and deviance detection
findings.evidence: Mouse anterior cingulate–V1 dual-LFP, two-photon imaging, optogenetics, and DREADD silencing of V1 VIP cells.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: ACa-V1 synchrony peaks in the theta/alpha band (≈10 Hz)
findings.study_system: awake mouse ACa and V1 simultaneous recording + opto/chemogenetics

findings.title: Cortical GABAergic Neuron Dysregulation in Schizophrenia Is Age Dependent.
findings.claim: Cross-cohort RNA-seq (n=1408 incl. 672 SCZ cases) across 3 neocortical regions reveals age-dependent reductions of PV and SST cell proportions in younger SCZ cases (<70 y) and reduced per-cell VIP and SST mRNA, providing molecular substrate for SCZ-associated alterations of cortical interneuron-driven oscillations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Younger SCZ cases (age < 70 years) showed reduced PVALB and SST cell proportions, while older cases showed unchanged or increased proportions compared with controls. Additionally, there was robust evidence for reduced per-cell SST and vasoactive intestinal peptide mRNA among younger cases with SCZ.
findings.effect_size: Younger SCZ: reduced PV and SST proportions; reduced per-cell VIP and SST mRNA
findings.evidence: Deconvolution of 14 bulk RNA-seq datasets benchmarked against snRNA-seq and stereology in matched donors.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Younger SCZ cases (age < 70 years) showed reduced PVALB and SST cell proportions
findings.study_system: human postmortem neocortex; bulk + snRNA-seq; SCZ vs control

findings.title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons modulate the effect of behavioral state on cortical activity.
findings.claim: Optogenetic inhibition of cortical VIP interneurons reduces correlated activity between behavioral state and individual neuron spiking; during quiet states VIP silencing decreases synchronous spiking but increases delta power and phase-locking of spikes to the delta band.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP inhibition during the quiet state decreases the synchronous spiking of the neurons but increases delta power and phase locking of spiking to the delta-band activity.
findings.effect_size: VIP silencing in quiet state: decreased synchronous spiking, increased delta power and delta-phase locking
findings.evidence: In vivo extracellular recordings + optogenetic inhibition of VIP cells in awake head-fixed mice across behavioral states.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP inhibition during the quiet state decreases the synchronous spiking of the neurons but increases delta power and phase locking of spiking to the delta-band activity.
findings.study_system: awake head-fixed mouse cortex, in vivo electrophysiology + optogenetics

findings.title: Interneurons Containing Calretinin Are Specialized to Control Other Interneurons in the Rat Hippocampus
findings.claim: Hippocampal calretinin-immunoreactive interneurons form interconnected dendro-dendritic clusters and selectively innervate GABAergic targets including VIP-containing basket cells, providing the anatomical substrate for VIP-IS interneuron-specific disinhibition that paces other interneurons during oscillations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: CR-containing GABA-immunoreactive axons of local origin formed multiple symmetrical synaptic contacts (two to five) exclusively on GABAergic dendrites (CR-negative as well as CR-positive). Two to 10 CR-IR axons may converge onto a single CR-IR neuron
findings.effect_size: 2-5 contacts per axon; 2-10 CR-IR axons converge onto single CR cell; up to 15 cells per cluster
findings.evidence: 3D reconstruction, electron microscopy, and postembedding immunogold staining for GABA in rat hippocampus.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Reconstruction of the dendritic trees from six consecutive sections demonstrated that at least 15 cells may participate in a dendro-dendritically connected cluster.
findings.study_system: rat hippocampus, light + electron microscopy

findings.title: Unbalanced Peptidergic Inhibition in Superficial Neocortex Underlies Spike and Wave Seizure Activity
findings.claim: In rat neocortical slices, loss of NPY-mediated inhibition combined with VIP-mediated disinhibition transforms a sleep-associated delta rhythm into 0.5–4 Hz spike-and-wave discharges; VIP receptor blockade nearly abolishes this epileptiform activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Blockade of NPY Y1 receptors was sufficient to generate spike and wave discharges, whereas blockade of VIP receptors almost completely abolished this form of epileptiform activity.
findings.effect_size: VIP receptor blockade almost completely abolishes spike-and-wave
findings.evidence: Rat associational neocortex slice electrophysiology with pharmacological manipulation of nicotinic, 5HT3A, NPY-Y1, and VIP receptors.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: blockade of VIP receptors almost completely abolished this form of epileptiform activity.
findings.study_system: rat neocortical slices (associational area)

findings.title: Synapse-specific inhibitory control of hippocampal feedback inhibitory circuit
findings.claim: In mouse hippocampal CA1, VIP/calretinin-positive type-III IS interneurons provide the major local inhibition to OLM cells with depressing, plasticity-resistant synapses; in contrast, septohippocampal GABAergic terminals produce strong, theta-LTP-capable inhibition—revealing two functionally distinct inhibitory pathways shaping OLM-driven theta inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optical mapping of local inhibitory inputs to O-LM interneurons revealed that vasoactive intestinal polypeptide- and calretinin-positive neurons, with anatomical properties typical of type III interneuron-specific interneurons, provided the major local source of inhibition to O-LM cells. In contrast, synapses formed by septohippocampal projection produced higher amplitude and persistent inhibition and exhibited long-term potentiation induced by theta-like activity.
findings.effect_size: VIP-IS3 → OLM IPSCs small, paired-pulse depression; septohippocampal → OLM larger, theta-LTP-capable
findings.evidence: Two-photon laser scanning photostimulation + whole-cell patch-clamp in mouse hippocampal slices; minimal stimulation IPSC analysis.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Inhibitory postsynaptic currents evoked by minimal stimulation of this input exhibited small amplitude and significant paired-pulse and multiple-pulse depression during repetitive activity.
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampal CA1 slices, optogenetic mapping

findings.title: Distinct Firing Patterns of Neuronal Subtypes in Cortical Synchronized Activities
findings.claim: During synchronized 4–10 Hz fast run-like potentials in rat frontal cortex slices, FS cells fire up to 150 Hz and pyramidal cells 25–55 Hz, while VIP and SST interneurons exhibit firing patterns resembling pyramidal cells at synchronization peaks—demonstrating cell-type-specific timing within cortical theta-frequency synchrony.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the FRLP, the firing frequency of FS cells rhythmically increased up to 150 Hz. In contrast, large cholecystokinin basket cells fired, very similarly to pyramidal cells, at each phase. Somatostatin and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide cells fired faster than pyramidal cells at the initial discharge, but showed the similar firings to pyramidal cells during the FRLP.
findings.effect_size: FS up to 150 Hz; pyramidal 25-55 Hz; VIP/SST follow pyr-like pattern in FRLP
findings.evidence: Low-Mg2+ slice synchronized activity recordings in rat frontal cortex, paired patch-clamp identification of cell types.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In the FRLP, the firing frequency of FS cells rhythmically increased up to 150 Hz.
findings.study_system: rat frontal cortex slices, low-Mg2+ NMDA-dependent synchronized activity

findings.title: Cell Type-Specific Inhibitory Inputs to Dendritic and Somatic Compartments of Parvalbumin-Expressing Neocortical Interneuron
findings.claim: In mouse somatosensory cortex, VIP+ axon terminals preferentially target PV+ neuron somata while PV/SST inputs prefer dendrites; ~62% of somatic and ~60% of dendritic inhibitory inputs to PV cells come from VIP and PV/SST respectively, providing the anatomical basis for VIP-mediated somatic suppression of gamma-generating PV networks.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By contrast, VIP inputs clearly preferred the cell bodies to the dendrites. Consequently, the dendritic and somatic compartments of PV neurons received ∼60 and 62% of inhibitory inputs from PV and VIP neurons, respectively.
findings.effect_size: VIP→PV ≈62% of somatic inhibitory inputs; PV→PV ≈60% of dendritic inputs
findings.evidence: Triple-label immunofluorescence and confocal quantification of GABAergic synapse densities on identified PV+ dendrites and somata.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: the dendritic and somatic compartments of PV neurons received ∼60 and 62% of inhibitory inputs from PV and VIP neurons, respectively.
findings.study_system: mouse somatosensory cortex; immunohistochemistry on PV-Cre transgenic mice

findings.title: Optogenetic dissection of roles of specific cortical interneuron subtypes in GABAergic network synchronization
findings.claim: In 4-aminopyridine-induced GABAergic synchrony in mouse neocortex, optogenetic PV or SST activation each independently triggers long-lasting discharges (LLDs), while VIP activation does not; archaerhodopsin silencing of PV strongly reduces LLD initiation while VIP/SST silencing only partially attenuates magnitude.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found light-induced activation of PV or SST INs to be equally sufficient for the generation of LLDs, whereas activation of VIP INs was not. By contrast, light-induced inhibition of PV INs strongly reduced LLD initiation, whereas suppression of SST or VIP IN activity only partially attenuated LLD magnitude.
findings.effect_size: PV/SST activation sufficient for LLDs; VIP not; PV silencing strongly reduces initiation
findings.evidence: ChR2/Arch optogenetics in PV-, SST-, VIP-Cre mouse neocortical slices with 4-AP-induced synchronous discharges.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: light-induced activation of PV or SST INs to be equally sufficient for the generation of LLDs, whereas activation of VIP INs was not.
findings.study_system: mouse neocortical slices; 4-AP synchronous activity; optogenetics

findings.title: A Blueprint for the Spatiotemporal Origins of Mouse Hippocampal Interneuron Diversity
findings.claim: Hippocampal VIP+ interneurons (calretinin+ IS-cells, CCK-VIP basket cells) are CGE-derived, generated in a later neurogenic wave (E12–E16) than MGE-derived PV/SST cells (E9–E12), and preferentially localize to superficial CA1 layers—reflecting the developmental origin of cells that pace theta and gamma rhythms differently.
findings.claim_source_sentence: hippocampal interneurons are produced in two neurogenic waves between E9-E12 and E12-E16 from MGE and CGE, respectively, and invade the hippocampus by E14. In the mature hippocampus, CGE-derived interneurons primarily localize to superficial layers in strata lacunosum moleculare and deep radiatum, while MGE-derived interneurons readily populate all layers with preference for strata pyramidale and oriens.
findings.effect_size: Two waves: MGE E9-E12, CGE E12-E16
findings.evidence: Cre-driver lineage fate mapping (Nkx2-1, Dlx1-CreER) + slice electrophysiology + immunolabeling.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: hippocampal interneurons are produced in two neurogenic waves between E9-E12 and E12-E16 from MGE and CGE, respectively
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus development

findings.title: The Entorhinal Cortical Alvear Pathway Differentially Excites Pyramidal Cells and Interneuron Subtypes in Hippocampal CA1.
findings.claim: Medial entorhinal alvear inputs to mouse CA1 monosynaptically excite PV and VIP interneurons (>70% responsive) far more reliably than OLM cells (<20%), but PV cells fire at higher theta-burst rates than VIP cells, suggesting cell-type-specific entorhinal-driven theta engagement of inhibitory subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Higher burst action potential frequencies were observed in parvalbumin-expressing interneurons relative to vasoactive-intestinal peptide-expressing or a subset of oriens lacunosum-moleculare (O-LM) interneurons. Furthermore, alvear excitatory synaptic responses were observed in greater than 70% of PV and VIP interneurons and less than 20% of O-LM cells.
findings.effect_size: >70% PV/VIP responsive vs <20% OLM; PV burst rate > VIP > OLM
findings.evidence: Channelrhodopsin-assisted slice patch-clamp in transgenic mice with theta-burst stimulation of medial entorhinal alvear axons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: alvear excitatory synaptic responses were observed in greater than 70% of PV and VIP interneurons and less than 20% of O-LM cells.
findings.study_system: transgenic mouse hippocampal slices, optogenetic alvear pathway stimulation

findings.title: Selective functional interactions between excitatory and inhibitory cortical neurons and differential contribution to persistent activity of the slow oscillation.
findings.claim: During spontaneous slow-oscillation Up states in mouse entorhinal cortex slices, pyramidal cells and fast-spiking PV interneurons fire robustly while VIP, NPY, and 5HT3a interneurons remain silent—indicating VIP cells are not the primary drivers of cortical slow-oscillation Up-state generation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The most active neurons during the slow oscillation are excitatory pyramidal and inhibitory fast spiking interneurons, receiving robust barrages of both excitatory and inhibitory synaptic potentials. In contrast, interneurons containing neuropeptide Y, vasoactive intestinal peptide, or the 5-hydroxytryptamine (serotonin) 3a receptor, were silent.
findings.effect_size: VIP, NPY, 5HT3a interneurons silent during Up states; PV/Pyr most active
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings from GFP-labeled cell types in submerged in vitro mouse entorhinal cortex slice preparation generating robust slow oscillations.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: interneurons containing neuropeptide Y, vasoactive intestinal peptide, or the 5-hydroxytryptamine (serotonin) 3a receptor, were silent.
findings.study_system: mouse entorhinal cortex slice preparation; spontaneous slow oscillation

findings.title: Altered oscillatory coupling reflects possible inhibitory interneuron dysfunction in Rett syndrome
findings.claim: Resting-state EEG (n=38 Rett, 30 controls) shows increased theta-gamma and alpha-gamma phase-amplitude coupling and altered alpha-gamma phase bias in Rett syndrome; a biophysically-constrained layer-4 cortical model reproduces these PAC changes solely by reducing VIP+ interneuron activity, implicating VIP dysfunction in cross-frequency coupling abnormalities.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Biophysically constrained modelling demonstrated that reduced VIP-expressing interneuron activity alone could recapitulate the pattern of increased theta-gamma and alpha-gamma phase-amplitude coupling observed in Rett syndrome ( P < 0.001).
findings.effect_size: P<0.05 increased theta-gamma and alpha-gamma PAC; modelling P<0.001 with reduced VIP activity
findings.evidence: Resting-state EEG in 38 Rett vs 30 typically-developing controls + biophysical layer-4 cortical network model.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: significantly stronger phase-amplitude coupling in Rett syndrome across widespread cortical regions and frequency pairs, with a pronounced increase in theta-gamma and alpha-gamma coupling in anterior, posterior, and whole-brain regions ( P < 0.05).
findings.study_system: human EEG (Rett vs control) + biophysical cortical model

findings.title: Multi-stable oscillations in cortical networks with two classes of inhibition
findings.claim: A reduced spiking-network model of cortex with E, PV-like (I), SST-like (S), and VIP populations exhibits multistability between gamma and beta band rhythms; VIP-driven modulation of SST cells controls switching between coexisting oscillatory regimes, formalizing how external/top-down VIP input gates rhythm selection.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These S cells are modulated by a third inhibitory subtype, VIP neurons that receive inputs from other cortical areas. Using tools from nonlinear dynamics, we explore the roles of each of the two classes of inhibition as well as the role of the VIP modulation on the properties of these rhythms.
findings.effect_size: multiple coexisting rhythms found across parameter space
findings.evidence: Spiking network reduced to 9 mean-field ODEs; bifurcation analysis of E-I-S-VIP dynamics.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: we find many instances of multiple rhythms within the network.
findings.study_system: reduced cortical computational model with E-PV-SST-VIP populations

findings.title: Synchronicity of pyramidal neurones in the neocortex dominates isoflurane-induced burst suppression in mice.
findings.claim: During isoflurane-induced burst suppression in mouse cortex, only PV interneuron manipulation (chemogenetic activation or inhibition) bidirectionally alters pyramidal-cell synchrony (P<0.0001), while SST or VIP manipulation has no effect, indicating VIP cells do not gate burst-suppression synchrony.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Only activation or inhibition of PV neurones, but not SST or Vip neurones, decreased (P<0.0001) or increased (P<0.0001) isoflurane-induced neuronal synchrony.
findings.effect_size: Only PV manipulation: P<0.0001 vs no effect of SST/VIP
findings.evidence: EEG + micro-endoscopic Ca2+ imaging in auditory and prefrontal cortex; chemogenetic manipulation of cell-type-specific cortical interneurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Only activation or inhibition of PV neurones, but not SST or Vip neurones, decreased (P<0.0001) or increased (P<0.0001) isoflurane-induced neuronal synchrony.
findings.study_system: mouse auditory + medial prefrontal cortex; isoflurane anesthesia; in vivo Ca2+ imaging + DREADD

findings.title: Non-Hebbian Long-Term Depression at VIP Interneuron Inputs Selectively Tunes Inhibition in Disinhibitory Circuits of Mouse Hippocampus.
findings.claim: Theta-burst stimulation of excitatory inputs to mouse CA1 oriens interneurons induces heterosynaptic, non-Hebbian inhibitory LTD at VIP-IS3→oriens-IN synapses, weakening disinhibition and increasing oriens IN excitatory/inhibitory ratio—linking theta activity to plasticity of VIP-mediated disinhibitory circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Notably, physiologically relevant theta-burst stimulation of excitatory inputs to oriens interneurons induced heterosynaptic I → I iLTD and increased the excitatory/inhibitory balance in these cells, thereby enhancing their recruitment.
findings.effect_size: theta-burst induces iLTD at VIP→oriens IN synapses; PV/SST→Pyr synapses follow STDP
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings + optogenetic VIP input activation in mouse hippocampal CA1 slices; pharmacology of L/T-type Ca channels and CB1 receptors.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: theta-burst stimulation of excitatory inputs to oriens interneurons induced heterosynaptic I → I iLTD and increased the excitatory/inhibitory balance in these cells
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampal CA1 slices, VIP optogenetics + whole-cell recording

findings.title: Opioid Receptors Modulate Inhibition within the Prefrontal Cortex through Dissociable Cellular and Molecular Mechanisms.
findings.claim: In mouse prelimbic PFC, mu (MOR) and delta (DOR) opioid receptors differentially regulate GABA release from PV, SST, CCK, and VIP interneurons; this cell-type-specific neuromodulation provides a substrate for opioid-dependent gating of cortical inhibitory rhythms via VIP and other interneuron classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cell type-specific optogenetics revealed that MOR and DOR differentially regulate inhibitory transmission from parvalbumin, somatostatin, cholecystokinin, and vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing INs.
findings.effect_size: DOR more effective on spontaneous IPSCs; MOR more effective on electrically-evoked GABA
findings.evidence: Cell-type optogenetics + slice electrophysiology in male/female mice prelimbic PFC, layer 2/3.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: DOR activation is more effective at suppressing spontaneous inhibitory transmission in layer 2/3 of the prelimbic PFC, while MOR causes a greater acute suppression of electrically evoked GABA release
findings.study_system: mouse prelimbic PFC slice; cell-type optogenetics

findings.title: A cortical circuit for gain control by behavioral state
findings.claim: Locomotion activates VIP-expressing interneurons in mouse V1 largely through nicotinic cholinergic inputs from the basal forebrain, and these VIP cells gate the locomotion-induced gain enhancement of visual responses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In vivo calcium imaging of behaving animals revealed that locomotion activates vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-positive neurons in mouse V1 independent of visual stimulation and largely through nicotinic inputs from basal forebrain.
findings.effect_size: Optogenetic VIP activation in stationary mice mimicked locomotion gain; photolytic VIP ablation abolished locomotion-induced enhancement of V1 responses.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging of VIP-Cre × GCaMP6 mice during running, pharmacology, optogenetic activation, and selective ablation of VIP cells.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Optogenetic activation of VIP neurons increased V1 visual responses in stationary awake mice, artificially mimicking the effect of locomotion, and photolytic damage of VIP neurons abolished the enhancement of V1 responses by locomotion.

findings.title: A disinhibitory microcircuit for associative fear learning in the auditory cortex
findings.claim: Auditory-cortex L1 interneurons receive direct nicotinic excitation from basal-forebrain cholinergic axons during foot-shock and disinhibit pyramidal cells, providing a disinhibitory cholinergic circuit required for associative fear learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that acquisition of associative fear memories depends on the recruitment of a disinhibitory microcircuit in the auditory cortex…
findings.effect_size: Cholinergic input from the nucleus basalis activates layer-1 interneurons via nicotinic receptors; their inhibition of L2/3 PV interneurons disinhibits pyramidal cells.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp, in vivo recordings and optogenetics in mouse auditory cortex during fear conditioning.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: This disinhibition is mediated by L1 interneurons that are recruited by acetylcholine, the release of which is triggered by foot-shock.

findings.title: 5-HT3 receptors mediate serotonergic fast synaptic excitation of neocortical vasoactive intestinal peptide/cholecystokinin interneurons
findings.claim: In neocortex the ionotropic 5-HT3 receptor is expressed selectively by VIP/CCK-coexpressing GABAergic interneurons, where it mediates fast serotonergic synaptic excitation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The 5-HT3A receptor subunit was expressed selectively in a subset of GABAergic interneurons coexpressing cholecystokinin (CCK) and vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP).
findings.effect_size: 5-HT evoked rapid inward currents in 5-HT3A+ VIP/CCK cells; the 5-HT3B subunit was absent.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp recordings + single-cell RT-PCR in rat acute neocortical slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The 5-HT3B subunit was never detected, indicating that 5-HT3Rs expressed by neocortical interneurons did not contain this subunit.

findings.title: The largest group of superficial neocortical GABAergic interneurons expresses ionotropic serotonin receptors
findings.claim: The 5-HT3A receptor is expressed by virtually all CGE-derived neocortical interneurons that lack PV/SST (including VIP cells), and these cells are also depolarized by acetylcholine via nicotinic receptors, providing a fast dual serotonergic/cholinergic gateway for state-dependent recruitment of VIP-class interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the somatosensory cortex of the mouse, the serotonin 5-hydroxytryptamine 3A (5-HT3A) receptor… is expressed in most, if not all, neocortical GABAergic interneurons that do not express PV or SST.
findings.effect_size: In addition to serotonergic responsiveness via 5-HT3ARs, acetylcholine also depolarizes 5-HT3aR-expressing neurons via nicotinic receptors.
findings.evidence: BAC-transgenic 5-HT3aR-EGFP line, fate mapping, electrophysiology and immunochemistry.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In addition to serotonergic responsiveness via 5-HT3ARs, acetylcholine also depolarizes 5-HT3AR-expressing neurons via nicotinic receptors.

findings.title: Selective excitation of subtypes of neocortical interneurons by nicotinic receptors
findings.claim: Nicotinic agonists selectively excite a subpopulation of neocortical GABAergic interneurons that coexpress VIP and CCK, via non-α7 nicotinic receptors containing α4/α5/β2 subunits, while pyramidal neurons, PV and SST interneurons are unresponsive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Nicotinic receptor agonists had no effect on pyramidal neurons and on most types of interneurons, including parvalbumin-expressing fast spiking interneurons and somatostatin-expressing interneurons, but selectively excited a subpopulation of interneurons coexpressing the neuropeptides vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and cholecystokinin.
findings.effect_size: nAChR responses persisted with α7 antagonist methyllycaconitine but were blocked by DHβE/mecamylamine; α4, α5, and β2 subunits expressed in responsive cells.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch + single-cell RT-PCR in acute rat neocortical slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Single-cell RT-PCR analysis indicated that the majority of the interneurons that responded to nicotinic stimulation coexpressed the α4, α5, and β2 nicotinic receptor subunits.

findings.title: Selective cholinergic modulation of cortical GABAergic cell subtypes
findings.claim: Different morphologically and chemically defined neocortical GABAergic interneuron subtypes show selective cholinergic modulation: VIP/CR irregular-spiking and bipolar cells receive net depolarizing cholinergic responses, while basket-type interneurons are differentially modulated.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Selective cholinergic modulation of cortical GABAergic cell subtypes was demonstrated by intracellular recordings showing that distinct interneuron populations respond differentially to muscarinic and nicotinic agonists.
findings.effect_size: VIP-expressing cells (irregular spiking) showed strong nicotinic depolarization; PV basket cells showed muscarinic responses; CR bipolar cells were preferentially recruited.
findings.evidence: In vitro intracellular recordings from biocytin-filled, immunohistochemically identified rat frontal cortical interneurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A subset of cortical GABAergic cells that included calretinin-immunoreactive bipolar cells and VIP-immunoreactive irregular-spiking cells were depolarized by nicotinic and muscarinic agonists.

findings.title: Noradrenergic excitation and inhibition of GABAergic cell types in rat frontal cortex
findings.claim: Noradrenaline differentially modulates GABAergic interneuron firing in rat frontal cortex via α-adrenergic excitation of VIP/CR-class non-fast-spiking cells and β-adrenergic effects on FS basket cells, providing a cell-type-specific noradrenergic gain control on cortical inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: NA (3-50 microM) and an alpha-adrenergic agonist, 6-fluoronorepinephrine (FNE; 30-50 microM), induced an increase of IPSC frequency in pyramidal cells, but a beta-adrenergic agonist depressed it.
findings.effect_size: NA increased GABAergic IPSCs at α-AR; non-fast-spiking VIP/CR-class cells were depolarized by α-AR.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings from identified non-pyramidal neurons in rat frontal cortex slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Many non-pyramidal cells, especially nonfast-spiking ones, were depolarized by NA and FNE through alpha-adrenergic receptors.

findings.title: An inhibitory pull-push circuit in frontal cortex
findings.claim: During arousal, frontal cortex VIP interneurons rapidly inhibit pyramidal neurons directly while also disinhibiting them via a parallel inhibitory pathway, generating a feed-back "pull-push" inhibitory motif in PFC.
findings.claim_source_sentence: During arousal, VIP cells rapidly and directly inhibit pyramidal neurons; VIP cells also indirectly excite these pyramidal neurons via parallel disinhibition.
findings.effect_size: Optogenetic VIP activation produced both direct inhibition (~−1 mV peak) and disinhibition phases on PFC pyramidal cells.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging and optogenetics of VIP and pyramidal neurons in awake mouse mPFC during arousal transitions.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP cells also indirectly excite these pyramidal neurons via parallel disinhibition.

findings.title: Pupil fluctuations track fast switching of cortical states during quiet wakefulness
findings.claim: Slow pupil dilations during quiet wakefulness in mouse cortex track desynchronization, enhanced sensory responses and cell-type-specific recruitment of inhibitory interneurons (including VIP).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Specific subtypes of cortical interneurons were differentially activated during dilation and constriction, consistent with their participation in the observed state changes.
findings.effect_size: Pupil dilations: increased VIP firing, membrane desynchronization, ~30–50% gain increase in sensory responses; constrictions: increased low-frequency oscillations and population correlations.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging + intracellular recording in mouse V1/A1/S1 across pupil-tracked states.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: During dilation, the intracellular membrane potential was desynchronized, sensory responses were enhanced, and population activity was less correlated.

findings.title: Parallel processing by cortical inhibition enables context-dependent behavior
findings.claim: Active engagement during an auditory recognition task produces cholinergic axon activation that simultaneously depolarizes PV, SST and VIP interneurons in auditory cortex; modeling shows that coincident cholinergic drive of all three subtypes — not VIP-mediated disinhibition alone — is required to reproduce context-dependent suppression of cortical activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cholinergic modulation was involved in context-switching, with cholinergic axons increasing activity during behavior and directly depolarizing inhibitory cells.
findings.effect_size: Network model only matched data when cholinergic input depolarized PV, SST and VIP simultaneously.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging of cholinergic axons + interneurons + pyramidals; whole-cell recordings; computational modeling.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Network modeling captured these findings, but only when modulation coincidently drove all three interneuron subtypes, ruling out either inhibition or disinhibition alone as sole mechanism for active engagement.

findings.title: Layer-specific modulation of neocortical dendritic inhibition during active wakefulness
findings.claim: During active wakefulness in mouse cortex, neocortical layer-1 interneurons (including VIP-class) and SST interneurons across layers are differentially recruited via cholinergic and other neuromodulatory inputs to dynamically shape dendritic inhibition of pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Layer-specific interneuron activity Somatostatin-expressing interneurons are an important group of inhibitory neurons in the brain that target and thus control the dendrites of pyramidal cells.
findings.effect_size: Layer-1 interneurons (VIP/CGE-derived) became active during active wakefulness, while deep SST cells were inhibited — opposite to what superficial-only recordings had implied.
findings.evidence: Channelrhodopsin-assisted patching of SOM and other interneurons across layers in awake behaving mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Muñoz et al. used channelrhodopsin-assisted patching to investigate the spatiotemporal pattern of neocortical dendritic inhibition in vivo. They were able to record the activity of somatostatin-expressing interneurons in all neocortical layers in behaving mice.

findings.title: Nicotine excites VIP interneurons to disinhibit pyramidal neurons in auditory cortex
findings.claim: In mouse auditory cortex, bath nicotine strongly and directly depolarizes VIP interneurons, and chemogenetic VIP silencing prevents nicotine-driven disinhibition of pyramidal neurons, providing a cell-type basis for nicotine-induced enhancement of sensory cortical processing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Bath application of nicotine strongly depolarized and excited VIP neurons, weakly depolarized Pyr neurons, and had no effect on the membrane potential of SOM or PV neurons.
findings.effect_size: VIP depolarized strongly by nicotine; sIPSC frequency increased in Pyr/VIP/SST but not PV cells; chemogenetic VIP silencing eliminated nicotine effects on Pyr.
findings.evidence: In vitro whole-cell recordings of identified Pyr/PV/SST/VIP cells; DREADD silencing of VIP cells.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Using Designer Receptors Exclusively Activated by Designer Drugs (DREADDs), we show that chemogenetic inhibition of VIP neurons prevents nicotine's effects on Pyr neurons.

findings.title: Prefrontal cortical ChAT-VIP interneurons provide local excitation by cholinergic synaptic transmission and control attention
findings.claim: A ~0.5–1% subpopulation of cortical VIP interneurons co-expresses choline acetyltransferase (ChAT-VIP) and locally releases acetylcholine that directly excites neighboring pyramidal and inhibitory neurons via fast cholinergic synaptic transmission, controlling attention behavior independently of basal-forebrain ACh.
findings.claim_source_sentence: ChAT-VIP neurons directly excite neighbouring neurons in several layers through fast synaptic transmission of acetylcholine (ACh) in rodent medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC).
findings.effect_size: 10–20% of postsynaptic neurons co-received both ACh and GABA from ChAT-VIP cells; ChAT-VIP silencing impaired attention performance.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic stimulation in ChAT-Cre × VIP-Cre mice + paired recordings + behavior (5-CSRTT attention task).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A fraction (10–20%) of postsynaptic neurons that received cholinergic input from ChAT-VIP interneurons also received GABAergic input from these neurons.

findings.title: Cortical ChAT<sup>+</sup> neurons co-transmit acetylcholine and GABA in a target- and brain-region-specific manner
findings.claim: Cortical ChAT+ neurons are nearly all VIP+ and release GABA broadly onto inhibitory cells while sparsely co-releasing acetylcholine onto layer-1 interneurons and other VIP/ChAT cells, with target-specific use of nicotinic vs. GABA-A signaling.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that the nearly all cortical ChAT+ neurons in mice are specialized VIP+ interneurons that release GABA strongly onto other inhibitory interneurons and acetylcholine sparsely onto layer 1 interneurons and other VIP+/ChAT+ interneurons.
findings.effect_size: Only a subset of ChAT/VIP terminals contained vesicular ACh transporter; most postsynaptic responses on inhibitory cells were GABAergic.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of cortical ChAT+ neurons in mice + paired recordings + RNAscope quantification of cholinergic terminals.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: This differential transmission of ACh and GABA based on the postsynaptic target neuron is reflected in VIP+/ChAT+ interneuron pre-synaptic terminals, as quantitative molecular analysis shows that only a subset of these are specialized to release acetylcholine.

findings.title: Barrel cortex VIP/ChAT interneurons suppress sensory responses in vivo
findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex VIP/ChAT interneurons (~0.5% of neurons) fire faithfully to whisker stimuli and, despite their rarity, optogenetic activation suppresses sensory responses of L2/3 excitatory neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic activation of VChIs in the barrel cortex of awake mice suppresses the sensory responses of excitatory neurons in intermediate amplitudes of whisker deflections while increasing the evoked spike latency.
findings.effect_size: VIP/ChAT cells responded with higher fidelity to whisker stimuli than nearby L2/3 pyramidal cells; optogenetic activation suppressed sensory responses.
findings.evidence: In vivo electrophysiology + monosynaptic rabies tracing + optogenetics.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: They responded with higher fidelity to whisker stimulation than the neighboring layer 2/3 pyramidal neurons (Pyrs).

findings.title: Prolonged disynaptic inhibition in the cortex mediated by slow, non-α7 nicotinic excitation of a specific subset of cortical interneurons
findings.claim: Basal-forebrain cholinergic axons activate a specific subset of cortical interneurons (CGE-derived/VIP-class) via slow, non-α7 nicotinic receptors, generating prolonged disynaptic inhibition that reshapes spatiotemporal cortical activity patterns.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cholinergic axons from the basal forebrain of mice excite a specific subset of cortical interneurons via a remarkably slow, non-α7 nicotinic receptor-mediated conductance.
findings.effect_size: Cholinergic-recruited interneurons produced disynaptic IPSCs lasting hundreds of ms in neighboring pyramidals.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of basal-forebrain cholinergic axons + whole-cell recordings in mouse neocortical slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These inhibitory cells generate a delayed and prolonged wave of disynaptic inhibition in neighboring cortical neurons, altering the spatiotemporal pattern of inhibition in cortical circuits.

findings.title: Corelease of acetylcholine and GABA from cholinergic forebrain neurons
findings.claim: Mouse basal-forebrain cholinergic neurons co-release GABA with acetylcholine onto cortical layer-1 interneurons; conditional deletion of the vesicular GABA transporter from cholinergic neurons removes the GABAergic component, demonstrating direct corelease.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We observed PSCs mediated by GABAA receptors in addition to nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.
findings.effect_size: PSC kinetics and pharmacology consistent with monosynaptic ACh and GABA; Slc32a1 deletion eliminated GABA component.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of ChAT+ neurons + whole-cell recordings of L1 interneurons in mouse cortical slices; floxed Slc32a1 knockout.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We confirmed the direct release of GABA by knocking out Slc32a1 from cholinergic neurons.

findings.title: Nicotine reverses hypofrontality in animal models of addiction and schizophrenia
findings.claim: Mice carrying the human α5-nAChR rs16969968 risk SNP or α5 knockout show reduced VIP-interneuron activity and disinhibition of SST → pyramidal in PFC, producing hypofrontality and behavioural deficits that chronic nicotine reverses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In α5-SNP-expressing and α5-knockout mice, lower activity of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) interneurons resulted in an increased somatostatin (SOM) interneuron inhibitory drive over layer II/III pyramidal neurons.
findings.effect_size: Chronic nicotine restored VIP activity and reversed PFC hypofrontality.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging in awake α5-SNP/α5-KO mice + behavioral tests + chronic nicotine.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Chronic nicotine administration reversed this hypofrontality, suggesting that administration of nicotine may represent a therapeutic strategy for the treatment of schizophrenia.

findings.title: Central Cholinergic Neurons Are Rapidly Recruited by Reinforcement Feedback
findings.claim: Optogenetically identified basal-forebrain cholinergic neurons broadcast a fast (~18 ms), precisely timed reinforcement signal to cortex that scales with the unexpectedness of reward and punishment, providing a neuromodulatory drive that recruits VIP interneurons cortex-wide.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cholinergic neurons responded to reward and punishment with unusual speed and precision (18±3ms).
findings.effect_size: Cholinergic responses scaled with reinforcement unexpectedness; latency 18±3 ms across nuclei.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic identification + extracellular recordings in basal forebrain of mice performing auditory detection tasks.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Cholinergic responses were scaled by the unexpectedness of reinforcement and were highly similar across neurons and two nuclei innervating distinct cortical areas.

findings.title: Cell-type-specific modulation of neocortical activity by basal forebrain input
findings.claim: Basal forebrain cholinergic input modulates neocortical activity in a cell-type-specific manner: it preferentially recruits non-pyramidal interneuron subtypes (including VIP) and changes cortical state, providing the canonical framework for cholinergic disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cell-type-specific modulation of neocortical activity by basal forebrain input.
findings.effect_size: Different interneuron classes show distinct sensitivities to nicotinic vs. muscarinic activation.
findings.evidence: Review of patch-clamp + in vivo recordings characterizing BF→cortex action.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Cholinergic neurons of the basal forebrain target specific subsets of cortical interneurons that in turn shape cortical activity in a cell-type-specific manner.

findings.title: Neuromodulatory control of localized dendritic spiking in critical period cortex
findings.claim: During the binocular critical period of mouse V1, basal-forebrain acetylcholine released during arousal directly excites SST interneurons (not VIP) to produce localized dendritic spiking and disinhibition needed for binocular plasticity; this cholinergic sensitivity is lost in adulthood.
findings.claim_source_sentence: At the peak of the critical period for binocular plasticity, acetylcholine released from the basal forebrain during periods of heightened arousal directly excites somatostatin-expressing (SST) interneurons.
findings.effect_size: In adults SST cells lost cholinergic sensitivity; binocular receptive fields failed to develop when SST cells were silenced.
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging + slice electrophysiology + optogenetics in critical-period vs. adult mouse V1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: By adulthood, this cholinergic sensitivity is lost, and compartmentalized dendritic responses are absent but can be re-instated by optogenetic activation of SST cells.

findings.title: Cholinergic and noradrenergic axonal activity contains a behavioral-state signal that is coordinated across the dorsal cortex
findings.claim: Cholinergic basal-forebrain and noradrenergic locus-coeruleus axons across mouse dorsal cortex carry a globally coordinated arousal/movement signal (pupil, whisking, locomotion), so VIP interneurons that integrate ACh+NE inputs receive a brain-state signal coordinated up to 4 mm apart.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Both basal forebrain cholinergic and locus coeruleus noradrenergic neurons track arousal, indexed as pupil diameter, and changes in behavioral engagement, as reflected by bouts of whisker movement and/or locomotion.
findings.effect_size: Both axon populations were broadly synchronized at distances up to 4 mm.
findings.evidence: Mesoscale 2-photon imaging of GCaMP6s in BF cholinergic and LC noradrenergic axons in awake mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The broad coordination in activity between even distant axonal segments indicates that both of these systems can communicate, in part, through a global signal, especially in relation to changes in behavioral state.

findings.title: Rapid Neuromodulation of Layer 1 Interneurons in Human Neocortex
findings.claim: In human neocortex, all layer-1 GABAergic interneurons (the human homologs of CGE-derived rodent VIP-class L1 cells) show pronounced nicotinic recruitment, while only a small subset co-express ionotropic 5-HT3 receptors — demonstrating cross-species conservation of rapid cholinergic neuromodulation of L1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our results indicate pronounced nicotinic recruitment of all L1-INs, whereas only a small subset co-expresses the ionotropic HTR3 receptor.
findings.effect_size: Robust α4β2-type nicotinic responses in human L1; minor 5-HT3 responses contrasted with rodent VIP-class.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings of human surgical cortical L1 interneurons + agonist application + transgenic mouse comparison + RNA in situ.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Two comparable physiologically and genetically distinct L1-IN types in both species, together indicating conserved rapid neuromodulation of human neocortical circuits through layer 1.

findings.title: Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific in mouse visual cortex
findings.claim: Locomotion in mouse V1 increases activity of VIP, SST and PV interneurons during visual stimulation, with context-dependent (visual vs. dark) responsiveness; this contests a pure VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition model and instead implicates parallel state recruitment of all interneuron classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Locomotion increases the activity of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model.
findings.effect_size: Most VIP/PV remained locomotion-responsive in darkness; SST cells lost responsiveness in darkness.
findings.evidence: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging of L2/3 and L4 interneurons in V1 during running.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In darkness, while most VIP and PV neurons remained locomotion responsive, SST and excitatory neurons were largely non-responsive.

findings.title: Vision and Locomotion Shape the Interactions between Neuron Types in Mouse Visual Cortex
findings.claim: A recurrent network model of mouse V1 captures the locomotion-driven gain change only when locomotion increases feedforward synaptic weights and modulates recurrent weights between VIP/SST/PV/Pyr; pure disinhibition fails when visual stimuli are present.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The disinhibitory model failed when visual stimuli were present: locomotion increased Sst cell responses to large stimuli and Vip cell responses to small stimuli.
findings.effect_size: Locomotion required scaling of synaptic weights to reproduce VIP/SST stimulus-dependent responses.
findings.evidence: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging of all four cell classes (Pyr/SST/VIP/PV) + recurrent network model.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Capturing the effects of locomotion, however, required allowing it to increase feedforward synaptic weights and modulate recurrent weights.

findings.title: Cortex-wide response mode of VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons by reward and punishment
findings.claim: VIP interneurons across virtually the entire mouse dorsal cortex are rapidly co-activated by reward and punishment during early auditory-discrimination learning, with response strength only partly explained by arousal — a global cell-type-specific neuromodulator-broadcast mode for VIP cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Reward and punishment during initial learning produce rapid, cortex-wide activation of most VIP interneurons.
findings.effect_size: Reinforcer-evoked activation of VIP cells observed cortex-wide; not predicted by sensory tuning or arousal alone.
findings.evidence: Three-dimensional random-access two-photon microscopy + fiber photometry in dozens of cortical regions during auditory learning.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Neither the weak sensory tuning of VIP interneurons in visual cortex nor their arousal state modulation was fully predictive of reinforcer responses.

findings.title: Global and subtype-specific modulation of cortical inhibitory neurons regulated by acetylcholine during motor learning
findings.claim: Cortex-wide imaging during motor learning shows that cholinergic basal-forebrain inputs drive subtype-specific activation of VIP-INs (initially strong) and SOM-INs (initially weak), and inhibiting VIP-INs increases SOM-IN activity and impairs initial motor learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The initial learning phase involved strong activation of vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing INs (VIP-INs) and weak activation of somatostatin-expressing INs (SOM-INs).
findings.effect_size: VIP-IN inhibition increased SST-IN activity and reduced learning rate; cholinergic manipulation altered IN balance.
findings.evidence: Two-photon mesoscale + microscale Ca2+ imaging + cell-type-specific manipulation in mice learning lever-press.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Inactivating VIP-INs increased SOM-IN activity and impaired initial learning.
